charter.mission: Freeze the bounded admission rules that decide which candidate breadth samples may enter future bounded collection for the current sample-breadth target features, without collecting samples now.
in_scope: priority ordering rules under sample limits
bounded exclusion rules for noisy or misaligned candidates
success_criteria: freeze a bounded breadth sample admission protocol
define admission priority rules under current sample limits
close the phase with a clear admission-gate readiness posture or bounded hold posture
stop_criteria: if admission gating drifts into actual sample collection
if admission rules cannot stay tied to the frozen breadth-entry design
if no bounded exclusion logic can be written without weakening current evidence standards
charter.handoff_condition: After the charter opens, only replay-independent admission-gate artifacts are allowed until a bounded collection-entry posture is explicit.

interpretation: V1.8A already answered where breadth evidence could legally come from, but not which candidates inside those pools are admissible.
The next lawful question is therefore admission gating, not immediate sample collection.
So V1.8B should open as a bounded breadth sample admission phase, not as a collection or promotion phase.
